instruction: In ~/Desktop/test_environment_hard, copy all files matching *failed.ipynb into ./fails while preserving directory hierarchy, but exclude any files under a tmp directory and any file whose basename starts with draft_. Then create ./fails/fails_manifest.txt containing sha256 and relative path (format: <sha256>  <relative_path_from_fails>) for copied .ipynb files only, sorted by relative path.

command: import pyautogui; import time; pyautogui.click({SCREEN_WIDTH_HALF}, {SCREEN_HEIGHT_HALF}); time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.hotkey('ctrl', 'alt', 't'); time.sleep(0.5)
import pyautogui; import time; time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.write('cd ~/Desktop/test_environment_hard'); time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.press('enter')

result.command:
cd ~/Desktop/test_environment_hard; ok=1; [ -f fails/projectA/run1/model_failed.ipynb ] || ok=0; [ -f fails/projectB/deep/eval_failed.ipynb ] || ok=0; [ ! -f fails/projectA/tmp/cache_failed.ipynb ] || ok=0; [ ! -f fails/projectB/tmp/deeper/secret_failed.ipynb ] || ok=0; [ ! -f fails/projectB/deep/draft_experiment_failed.ipynb ] || ok=0; [ -f fails/fails_manifest.txt ] || ok=0; expected=$(printf '%s
' "$(sha256sum fails/projectA/run1/model_failed.ipynb | awk '{print $1}')  projectA/run1/model_failed.ipynb" "$(sha256sum fails/projectB/deep/eval_failed.ipynb | awk '{print $1}')  projectB/deep/eval_failed.ipynb" | sort); actual=$(cat fails/fails_manifest.txt 2>/dev/null); if [ "$actual" != "$expected" ]; then ok=0; fi; if [ "$ok" = "1" ]; then echo SUCCESS: hard copy task passed.; else echo FAIL: hard copy task failed.; fi
